task_completion.description: The 'Baguette Calculator' tiddler is created and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tiddler with the exact title 'Baguette Calculator' is visible in the story river (the main viewing area of TiddlyWiki).

task_completion.description: An input field for the batch multiplier is present in the tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An interactive input box (text field) is visible within the 'Baguette Calculator' tiddler, allowing the user to input or change the batch multiplier.

task_completion.description: The tiddler displays a list of all ingredients for the Baguette recipe.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The names of the four ingredients ('Bread Flour', 'Water', 'Salt', 'Instant Yeast') are clearly listed in the rendered view of the 'Baguette Calculator' tiddler.

task_completion.description: The tiddler displays the correctly scaled amounts for each ingredient based on the multiplier.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numeric values are shown for each ingredient. If the multiplier input is '1', the amounts must be 1000 for Bread Flour, 700 for Water, 20 for Salt, and 7 for Instant Yeast. If the multiplier is another value (e.g., 2), the amounts must be proportionally scaled (e.g., 2000, 1400, 40, 14).

task_completion.description: The tiddler displays the units for each ingredient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The appropriate unit (e.g., 'g') is displayed next to the scaled numeric amount for each ingredient in the list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the TiddlyWiki application via a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interface with TiddlyWiki loaded, rather than a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used TiddlyWiki widgets/macros rather than hardcoding the final text.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the tiddler is visible in edit mode, the text area should contain TiddlyWiki wikitext (like <$list>, <$edit-text>, or filter expressions) instead of plain hardcoded text like 'Bread Flour 1000g'.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'curl' or 'wget' being used to inject the tiddler directly into the backend.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected tiddler title is exactly 'Baguette Calculator'. The state tiddler used for the multiplier must be '$:/state/baguette-yield'. The base amounts for the ingredients (when multiplier is 1) are: Bread Flour = 1000, Water = 700, Salt = 20, Instant Yeast = 7.
